prompts: An educational guide on freshwater fish would include species such as trout, bass, catfish, pike, carp, and perch. Trout are known for their colorful spots and live in cold, clear streams. Bass, often found in lakes and rivers, are popular among anglers for their fighting spirit. Catfish are recognizable by their whisker-like barbels and nocturnal habits. Pike are predatory, with elongated bodies and sharp teeth. Carp are hardy fish that can thrive in diverse environments, often marked by their large scales. Perch are small, schooling fish found in lakes and rivers, known for their striped appearance.
